prompt:
You are Agent 4 of 4 working on the TaskFlow Pro project at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-bJrBsV.

Three previous agents have built a notification system with EventBus, multi-channel support, and priority-based routing.

Your task:
1. Read the current codebase to understand all components before writing anything.
2. Verify that priority-based routing is present (urgent→SMS, normal→email, low→webhook).
3. Add an audit logging service (e.g., src/services/audit.service.ts) that records every notification dispatched.
4. Add a notification preferences service (e.g., src/services/notification-preferences.service.ts) that allows per-user channel preferences to override the priority router.
5. Write integration tests that exercise the full notification flow: priority routing → channel selection → audit log entry.
6. Ensure all existing unit tests still pass.

parameters.task: Add audit logging service, notification preferences service, and integration tests for the full notification flow

parameters.description: List all files in the project

parameters.summary: Add AuditService and NotificationPreferencesService as optional dependencies of NotificationService, with a channel registry for preference-based routing
parameters.context: Need audit logging for every dispatched notification and per-user channel preferences that override priority routing. NotificationService currently only has PriorityRouter for dispatch.
parameters.rationale: AuditService is a standalone observer that records dispatched notifications. NotificationPreferencesService stores per-user (userId, priority) → channelType overrides. A channelRegistry Map maps channel type names to channel instances, allowing preference lookups to resolve to concrete channels. Both are optional constructor params to preserve backward compatibility with existing tests. Flow: check preferences → if override exists use channel registry → else use PriorityRouter → then log to AuditService.

alternatives.option: Use EventBus to emit audit events and have a separate listener
alternatives.reason_rejected: Over-engineered for simple in-memory audit logging; adds indirection without benefit
Violates single responsibility; router should only map priority→channel, not know about users

parameters.summary: Agent-4: Added AuditService, NotificationPreferencesService, and integration tests. All 115 tests pass.
parameters.detail:
Changes made:
- Created src/services/audit.service.ts: AuditService class that records every dispatched notification with id, timestamp, type, recipient, subject, body, priority, and channel used
- Created src/services/notification-preferences.service.ts: NotificationPreferencesService with per-user (userId, priority) → channelType overrides
- Updated src/services/notification.service.ts: Added optional auditService and preferencesService constructor params, channelRegistry for preference-based routing, sendNotification now checks user preferences before priority routing and logs to audit
- Created tests/services/audit.service.test.ts (7 tests)
- Created tests/services/notification-preferences.service.test.ts (11 tests)
- Created tests/integration/notification-flow.test.ts (10 tests covering full flow: priority routing → channel selection → audit log, user preference overrides, and end-to-end lifecycle)
- All 115 tests pass across 13 test files (was 87 tests across 10 files)
- TypeScript compiles cleanly
